etymology.description: 浴(浴) (yù, "bathe") + 室(室) (shì, "room") = a room for bathing
etymology.interpretation: A 浴室(浴室) (yùshì, "bathroom") combines 浴(浴) (yù, "bathe") and 室(室) (shì, "room") to describe a room that is specifically designated for bathing activities. This combination is straightforward and commonly understood in modern contexts as a place equipped for personal hygiene.

examples.chinese: 我每天早上在浴室(浴室)洗澡(洗澡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshang zài yùshì xǐzǎo.
examples.english: I take a shower in the bathroom every morning.

examples.chinese: 浴室(浴室)的墙壁(牆壁)是蓝色的(是藍色的)。
examples.pinyin: Yùshì de qiángbì shì lánsè de.
examples.english: The walls of the bathroom are blue.

examples.chinese: 他在装修浴室(他在裝修浴室)，所以现在不能使用(所以現在不能使用)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài zhuāngxiū yùshì, suǒyǐ xiànzài bùnéng shǐyòng.
examples.english: He is renovating the bathroom, so it can't be used right now.
